1.1-01.back: The repeating process by which the CPU fetches an instruction from Main memory (RAM), decodes it to work out what to do, then executes it — one instruction at a time, for as long as a program runs.

1.1-02a.front: What happens during the Fetch stage?
1.1-02a.back: The CPU fetches (retrieves) the next instruction from a specific location in memory (RAM). The instruction is held in the Memory Data Register (MDR).

1.1-02b.front: What happens during the Decode stage?
1.1-02b.back: The CPU decodes the instruction using the Control Unit (CU) — working out what it means and what needs to be done.

1.1-02c.front: What happens during the Execute stage?
1.1-02c.back: The CPU executes the instruction — carries it out. This may include the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) carrying out Maths or logic.

1.1-03-alu.front: What is the role of the ALU during the fetch-execute cycle?
1.1-03-alu.back: It carries out arithmetic and logical operations.

1.1-03-cu.front: What is the role of the CU (Control Unit) during the fetch-execute cycle?
1.1-03-cu.back: Controls the execution of instructions in the correct sequence. It decodes instructions. It sends and receives control signals to and from other devices within the computer.

1.1-03-cache.front: What is the role of Cache during the fetch-execute cycle?
1.1-03-cache.back: It stores frequently-used instructions and data close to the CPU, so they can be accessed faster than fetching from main memory.

1.1-03-reg.back: Temporary storage location in the CPU.

1.1-04.back: An architecture where both program instructions and data are held together in the same main memory (RAM), and are fetched, decoded and executed by the CPU.

1.1-04a.front: What does the MAR (Memory Address Register) store?
1.1-04a.back: The address of the instruction or data to be fetched or stored.

1.1-04b.front: What does the MDR (Memory Data Register) store?
1.1-04b.back: The data or instruction itself, once it's been fetched from memory.

1.1-04c.front: What does the Program Counter do?
1.1-04c.back: Stores the address of the next instruction to be processed. Increments every cycle.

1.1-04d.back: The results of operations carried out by the CPU.
